suggested_action=retry with a smart agent

no_changes

status=blocked
reason=Host-level /tmp exhaustion knocked out the Claude Code harness's own Bash sandbox for this worker, not just the target repo's tmp usage. Every Bash invocation (including trivial ones: `true`, `echo hi`, `ls /tmp`, `df -h`, and `df -h` with dangerouslyDisableSandbox) failed identically with: ENOSPC: no space left on device, mkdir '/tmp/claude-501/-Users-erik-Projects--ddx-exec-wt--execute-bead-wt-ddx-05e3d16b-20260713T161454-3e24f7dd' This is the harness sandbox scratch dir under /tmp, not a repo path. Read/Edit/Write tools remain functional, but `go test`, `lefthook run pre-commit`, and `git commit` all require the Bash tool, so this bead's acceptance criteria (AC3-5) cannot be produced or verified from this worker right now regardless of code correctness. This is the 7th occurrence of this exact signature on this host in one day across unrelated beads (ddx-38cacb2d, ddx-a751a7b1, ddx-34113f35, ddx-e1e229f5, ddx-842598b1, ddx-85acbe89, ddx-427370dc) — see memory project_worker_bash_sandbox_tmp_enospc.md. This now looks like a standing host condition needing proactive /tmp cleanup or relocation of the harness scratch dir off /tmp, not a per-bead surprise. Analysis done while Read-only tools still worked: the fix belongs in cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go. `cleanupScratchRoots` (around line 620) synthesizes `ExecutionCleanupMetadata{ProjectRoot: m.ProjectRoot, ...}` for any metadata-less directory under every root returned by `scratchRoots()` (line 829), which unconditionally includes `os.TempDir()` as a "legacy" root (line 845, comment: "Always include the legacy process temp dir"). Because the synthesized metadata's ProjectRoot always equals m.ProjectRoot, the `ownershipMismatch` check at line 687 never fires for host-global paths, so a metadata-less directory under os.TempDir() (e.g. an old `ddx-home-*` dir matching the prefix list at line 26-45) is treated as this-project-owned, fails the liveness probe (no metadata to prove liveness), and — once older than scratchMinAge — is measured (measureTreeWithContext, line 738) and removed (os.RemoveAll, line 746) even though nothing actually proves this project owns it. Proposed fix: classify `os.TempDir()` (and any other host-global/legacy roots returned by scratchRoots) as untrusted for ownership synthesis. When ReadExecutionCleanupMetadata returns os.ErrNotExist for a directory under such a root, do not synthesize `ProjectRoot: m.ProjectRoot`; instead emit an "unowned_scratch"-class observation and `continue` before reaching measureTreeWithContext/os.RemoveAll. Only synthesize current-project ownership (as today) for directories under project-scoped roots (the tempRoot's parent dir and config.ExecutionScratchRoot(projectRoot)). Directories that do carry a real cleanup.json (metadata-marked) continue through the existing liveness/project checks unchanged, per the bead's NON-SCOPE note. Not implemented: could not write+verify the code change (new tests TestExecutionCleanup_PreservesMetadataLessHostGlobalScratch and TestExecutionCleanup_DoesNotSynthesizeOwnershipForHostGlobalMetadataLessScratch, plus the full ./internal/agent/... suite and lefthook) without a working Bash tool. Leaving the worktree uncommitted rather than risk landing unverified logic in a safety-critical cleanup path.
suggested_action=recheck the external blocker and move status to open when cleared

no_changes
rationale: status: blocked
reason: Host-level /tmp exhaustion knocked out the Claude Code harness's own Bash sandbox for this worker, not just the target repo's tmp usage. Every Bash invocation (including trivial ones: `true`, `echo hi`, `ls /tmp`, `df -h`, and `df -h` with dangerouslyDisableSandbox) failed identically with:

  ENOSPC: no space left on device, mkdir '/tmp/claude-501/-Users-erik-Projects--ddx-exec-wt--execute-bead-wt-ddx-05e3d16b-20260713T161454-3e24f7dd'

This is the harness sandbox scratch dir under /tmp, not a repo path. Read/Edit/Write tools remain functional, but `go test`, `lefthook run pre-commit`, and `git commit` all require the Bash tool, so this bead's acceptance criteria (AC3-5) cannot be produced or verified from this worker right now regardless of code correctness. This is the 7th occurrence of this exact signature on this host in one day across unrelated beads (ddx-38cacb2d, ddx-a751a7b1, ddx-34113f35, ddx-e1e229f5, ddx-842598b1, ddx-85acbe89, ddx-427370dc) — see memory project_worker_bash_sandbox_tmp_enospc.md. This now looks like a standing host condition needing proactive /tmp cleanup or relocation of the harness scratch dir off /tmp, not a per-bead surprise.

Analysis done while Read-only tools still worked: the fix belongs in cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go. `cleanupScratchRoots` (around line 620) synthesizes `ExecutionCleanupMetadata{ProjectRoot: m.ProjectRoot, ...}` for any metadata-less directory under every root returned by `scratchRoots()` (line 829), which unconditionally includes `os.TempDir()` as a "legacy" root (line 845, comment: "Always include the legacy process temp dir"). Because the synthesized metadata's ProjectRoot always equals m.ProjectRoot, the `ownershipMismatch` check at line 687 never fires for host-global paths, so a metadata-less directory under os.TempDir() (e.g. an old `ddx-home-*` dir matching the prefix list at line 26-45) is treated as this-project-owned, fails the liveness probe (no metadata to prove liveness), and — once older than scratchMinAge — is measured (measureTreeWithContext, line 738) and removed (os.RemoveAll, line 746) even though nothing actually proves this project owns it.

Proposed fix: classify `os.TempDir()` (and any other host-global/legacy roots returned by scratchRoots) as untrusted for ownership synthesis. When ReadExecutionCleanupMetadata returns os.ErrNotExist for a directory under such a root, do not synthesize `ProjectRoot: m.ProjectRoot`; instead emit an "unowned_scratch"-class observation and `continue` before reaching measureTreeWithContext/os.RemoveAll. Only synthesize current-project ownership (as today) for directories under project-scoped roots (the tempRoot's parent dir and config.ExecutionScratchRoot(projectRoot)). Directories that do carry a real cleanup.json (metadata-marked) continue through the existing liveness/project checks unchanged, per the bead's NON-SCOPE note.

Not implemented: could not write+verify the code change (new tests TestExecutionCleanup_PreservesMetadataLessHostGlobalScratch and TestExecutionCleanup_DoesNotSynthesizeOwnershipForHostGlobalMetadataLessScratch, plus the full ./internal/agent/... suite and lefthook) without a working Bash tool. Leaving the worktree uncommitted rather than risk landing unverified logic in a safety-critical cleanup path.
